mapper.insert: INSERT INTO sellers ( user_id, company_name, business_number, representative_name, opening_date, business_address ) VALUES ( #{user_id}, #{company_name}, #{business_number}, #{representative_name}, #{opening_date}, #{business_address} )
mapper.update: UPDATE sellers SET company_name = #{company_name}, business_number = #{business_number}, representative_name = #{representative_name}, opening_date = #{opening_date}, business_address = #{business_address} WHERE user_id = #{user_id}
mapper.select: SELECT COUNT(CASE WHEN oi.status = 'PAID' THEN 1 END) AS newOrderCount, COUNT(CASE WHEN oi.status = 'PREPARING' THEN 1 END) AS preparingCount, COUNT(CASE WHEN oi.status = 'SHIPPING' THEN 1 END) AS shippingCount, COUNT(CASE WHEN oi.status = 'DELIVERED' THEN 1 END) AS deliveredCount, COUNT(CASE WHEN oi.status = 'CONFIRMED' THEN 1 END) AS confirmedCount, COUNT(CASE WHEN oi.status = 'CANCELLED' THEN 1 END) AS cancelledCount FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON p.product_id = oi.product_id WHERE p.seller_id = #{seller_id}

SELECT COUNT(CASE WHEN status = 'APPROVED' AND stock > 0 THEN 1 END) AS onSaleCount, COUNT(CASE WHEN status = 'APPROVED' AND stock = 0 THEN 1 END) AS outOfStockCount FROM products WHERE seller_id = #{seller_id}
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM qnas q JOIN products p ON p.product_id = q.product_id WHERE p.seller_id = #{seller_id} AND q.answered_at IS NULL
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM reviews r JOIN products p ON p.product_id = r.product_id WHERE p.seller_id = #{seller_id}
mapper.update: UPDATE order_items SET status = #{status}, updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE order_item_id = #{order_item_id} AND product_id IN ( SELECT product_id FROM products WHERE seller_id = #{seller_id} ) AND ( (#{status} = 'PREPARING' AND status = 'PAID') OR (#{status} = 'SHIPPING' AND status = 'PREPARING') OR (#{status} = 'DELIVERED' AND status = 'SHIPPING') )
mapper.select: SELECT s.seller_id, s.user_id, s.company_name, s.business_number, s.representative_name, s.opening_date, s.business_address, u.photo_name, IFNULL(AVG(r.rating), 0) AS review_avg, COUNT(DISTINCT r.review_id) AS review_count, COUNT(DISTINCT f.product_id) AS product_favorite_count FROM sellers s JOIN users u ON s.user_id = u.user_id LEFT JOIN products p ON p.seller_id = s.seller_id LEFT JOIN reviews r ON r.product_id = p.product_id LEFT JOIN favorites f ON f.product_id = p.product_id WHERE s.seller_id = #{seller_id} GROUP BY s.seller_id, s.user_id, s.company_name, s.business_number, s.representative_name, s.opening_date, s.business_address, u.photo_name
